nodes.label: keybindings
nodes.summary: This project manages the setup of user **keybindings** by providing a specific CLI command. It ensures the feature is enabled via **feature gating** before safely creating a configuration file (preventing accidental data loss) and opening it in the user's preferred *external text editor*. The system is optimized to load the heavy execution logic **lazily**, only when the specific command is requested.

children.label: Command Module Structure
children.summary:
This abstraction defines how a distinct CLI action is structured. It separates the "metadata" (name, description, capabilities) from the actual "execution logic."

Think of it like a menu item in a restaurant. The `index.ts` is the text on the menu (Title: "Steak", Description: "Grilled to order"), telling the waiter what is available. The `keybindings.ts` is the recipe in the kitchen that details the actual steps the chef takes to cook the meal when ordered.

children.label: Feature Gating
children.summary:
This abstraction controls access to functionality based on configuration or environment states. Before the code executes logic or even advertises itself as available, it checks `isKeybindingCustomizationEnabled`.

It is like a VIP rope at a club. The bouncer checks if the "customization" feature is on the guest list. If it isn't, the command either hides itself (in `index.ts`) or politely informs the user they cannot proceed (in `keybindings.ts`), ensuring users don't access unfinished or disabled features.

children.label: Safe Resource Initialization
children.summary:
This abstraction handles the creation of configuration files using a "check-and-set" or "exclusive create" strategy. It uses the `wx` file system flag to attempt writing a file only if it doesn't already exist.

Think of it like reserving a seat at a movie theater. You don't just sit down blindly (which might result in sitting on someone else). You check the seat map; if the seat is empty, you claim it immediately. If it's taken (EEXIST error), you acknowledge it and proceed without trying to build a new chair on top of the old one.

children.label: External Editor Delegation
children.summary:
This abstraction hands off user interaction from the command line interface to the user's preferred text editor. It pauses the CLI workflow to allow complex editing in a specialized tool.

It is similar to a general contractor building a house. When it comes time to do the wiring, the contractor doesn't do it themselves; they call in a specialized electrician (the user's editor). The contractor waits until the electrician finishes their specific task before marking the job as complete.

children.label: Lazy Module Loading
children.summary:
This pattern ensures that heavy code is only imported into memory when the user actually requests it. In `index.ts`, the `load` property uses a dynamic import function.

Imagine a library where the librarian doesn't carry every book around constantly. Instead, they keep an index card (metadata). Only when a patron asks for a specific book does the librarian walk to the shelf to retrieve the actual content. This keeps the librarian (the application start-up) fast and unburdened.
